title: pr-minder config
description: Configuration for the pr-minder GitHub App. Supports JSONC (comments allowed). Place at .github/config/pr-minder/pr-minder.jsonc in any repo, or at the same path in the org's .github repo (with optional per-repo overrides under 'repos').

$schema.description: URL to this JSON Schema. Used by IDEs for validation and autocomplete.

repos.description: Per-repo config overrides. Only meaningful in the org-level .github/config/pr-minder/pr-minder.jsonc; ignored in per-repo config files.

triggerCondition.description: A single trigger condition. All specified keys must match (AND). Multiple conditions in the triggers array are ORed.

approved_by.description: PR must be approved by at least one of these GitHub usernames.

min_approvals.description: PR must have at least this many approvals from any users.

autoUpdatePr.description: Approval-gate settings for branch updates. Any passing condition fires update-branch.

triggers.description: List of trigger conditions (ORed). Keys within a condition are ANDed.

autoTriggerWorkflows.description: Re-trigger CI for 'zombie' PRs that have no workflow runs of their own. A PR created with the default GITHUB_TOKEN (author github-actions[bot]) never triggers its workflows (GitHub suppresses recursive runs); on the `opened` event pr-minder revives those. On the `reopened` event it revives any open PR that still has zero workflow runs, whatever its author. The fix is a close+reopen with the App's own credentials, which fires a fresh `pull_request.reopened` event that runs the workflows. Opt-in; defaults to false.

autoOpenPr.description: Automatically open a pull request for any branch that is ahead of its base and has no open PR. PRs are opened with pr-minder's own App credentials, so (unlike PRs created by a workflow's default GITHUB_TOKEN) they trigger their workflows normally and are never 'zombie' PRs. Fires on a push to a non-default branch, and sweeps existing branches when the App is installed or repositories are added. The default branch and gh-pages are always skipped. The base is target_base (or the default branch) unless base_from_fork_point is enabled, which instead targets the branch each branch was forked from.

enabled.description: Turn the feature on. Opt-in; defaults to false.

skip_branches.description: Additional branch names (exact match) to never open PRs for. The default branch and gh-pages are always skipped regardless.

skip_branch_patterns.description: Additional branches to never open PRs for, as regular-expression patterns matched against the branch name (anchor with ^...$ for a full-name match). Use this to exclude a whole family of branches at once — e.g. ["^\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+$"] excludes version branches like 2.1.81, so they never each become a PR head. A malformed pattern simply never matches.

target_base.description: Base branch for opened PRs. Defaults to the repository's default branch. When base_from_fork_point is enabled this is the fallback base used when no fork point is detected.

base_from_fork_point.description: Detect each branch's base from its fork point instead of always using target_base: pr-minder walks the branch's commits newest-first and uses the first ancestor that is the HEAD of another qualifying branch (the default branch, or one matching base_branch_patterns). In an archive repo a working branch forked from a long-lived branch (e.g. a version branch) is opened back into that branch; a branch forked off the default branch goes into the default branch. Falls back to target_base when no qualifying fork point is found. Opt-in; defaults to false.

base_branch_patterns.description: When base_from_fork_point is enabled, which non-default branches may be selected as a base, as regular-expression patterns matched against the branch name. The repository's default branch always qualifies; a non-default branch qualifies only if it matches one of these. E.g. ["^\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+$"] lets version branches be auto-detected bases.

autoDescribePr.description: Generate the PR title and description from the PR's full diff with an AI model, whenever a PR is opened (or marked ready for review) and on every new commit. The description is rewritten each time the diff changes (the existing description is passed to the model so still-accurate notes are preserved); the title is replaced only when the existing one is a missing/autogenerated placeholder. The model endpoint and credentials are configured on the Worker, not here.

enabled.description: Turn the feature on. Opt-in; defaults to false.

model.description: Override the default model name for this repo/org (the default is configured on the pr-describe webhook).

labelOptions.description: Settings for a single label name.

auto_add.description: If 'on_pr_creation', automatically apply this label to PRs when they are opened. 'false' or omitted disables auto-add.

create_label_if_missing_in_repo.description: If true, create this label in the repo (with the configured color) if it does not already exist.

color.description: Hex color used when creating this label, with or without a leading '#'.

mode.description: 'auto_update': PR branch is kept in sync with its base whenever this label is present (complementary to auto_update_pr.triggers). 'auto_merge': adding/removing this label enables/disables GitHub's native auto-merge; toggling auto-merge via the GitHub UI adds/removes the label.

auto_merge_method.description: Merge method to use when enabling auto-merge. Only relevant when mode is 'auto_merge'.

autoLabelPr.description: Map of label name to its settings.

repoConfig.description: Per-repo overrides. Supports the same fields as the top-level config, except 'repos'.
